input.target_audience: Pixel visitors who visited the pricing page in the last 60 days and did not convert. Secondary: video viewers (75%+ watch time) from the last 30 days.
ad_copy_variants: You checked out our pricing. Here's what you get with the Pro plan — and why 4,000 developers switched this year.
Still evaluating? We'll set you up with a 14-day trial, no card required. Takes 90 seconds.

output:
## Meta Campaign Blueprint: GitPilot — Developer Tool Retargeting

### Campaign Overview
**Campaign Name:** GitPilot | Retargeting | Pricing Visitors + Video Viewers | Oct 2025
**Objective:** Conversions (Website — Trial Signup)
**Budget:** $3,000/month | ABO recommended
**Estimated CPL/CPA Range:** $35–$70 per trial signup (retargeting benchmarks for developer tools)

---

### Objective Rationale
Conversions objective is correct here. These are warm audiences — pricing page visitors have already demonstrated purchase intent; they know the product and evaluated cost. Driving them back to the site with a low-friction trial offer (no credit card) is the right mechanism. Instant Forms would lose context (they need to land on the trial page to understand value). The Pixel must be firing a 'Lead' or custom 'TrialStart' event on the /trial confirmation page. Confirm 50+ events/week before letting the campaign exit learning phase — at $3k/month this may take 3–4 weeks for the algorithm to stabilize.

**Important:** With a $3,000/month budget split across 2 retargeting ad sets, individual ad set daily budgets are modest ($50–$70/day). At this scale, avoid splitting audiences too granularly — two ad sets maximum.

---

### Ad Set Structure (ABO)

#### Ad Set 1: Retargeting | Pricing Page Visitors (60 Days) — Excludes Converters
- **Audience Type:** Retargeting
- **Targeting:** Pixel visitors: URL contains '/pricing' in last 60 days. EXCLUDE: anyone who triggered 'Lead' or 'Purchase' pixel event in last 60 days (already converted).
- **Geography:** United States, Canada, United Kingdom, Australia (English-speaking developer markets)
- **Estimated Audience Size:** Depends on site traffic. Requires minimum 1,000 users. If pool < 1,000, extend lookback window to 90 days or merge with Ad Set 2.
- **Budget Allocation:** 65% = $1,950/month ($65/day)
- **Placements:** Facebook Feed, Instagram Feed, Facebook Right Column (desktop developers). Audience Network: EXCLUDED.
- **Frequency Cap:** 3 impressions/user/week (warm audience — can tolerate slightly higher frequency)
- **Ad Variants Assigned:** Variant A (you-checked-pricing), Variant C (what-you-missed)

#### Ad Set 2: Retargeting | Video Viewers 75%+ (30 Days)
- **Audience Type:** Retargeting
- **Targeting:** Users who watched 75% or more of any GitPilot video ad or organic video post in the last 30 days. EXCLUDE: Pricing page visitors (already in Ad Set 1 — no overlap).
- **Geography:** United States, Canada, United Kingdom, Australia
- **Estimated Audience Size:** Highly variable — requires active video content running. Minimum 1,000 users required.
- **Budget Allocation:** 35% = $1,050/month ($35/day)
- **Placements:** Facebook Feed, Instagram Feed, Instagram Stories (video viewers respond well to Stories format as it matches their prior behavior)
- **Frequency Cap:** 3 impressions/user/week
- **Ad Variants Assigned:** Variant B (14-day trial, no card required)

---

### CBO vs. ABO Recommendation
**Use ABO (Ad Set Budget Optimization).**

Rationale: At $3k/month with two retargeting pools, CBO will concentrate spend on whichever audience has more daily available impressions — likely the pricing visitors pool if it's larger. This would underserve the video viewers segment, which may have a different buyer profile worth understanding independently. ABO also ensures the $35/day floor on Ad Set 2 is respected. If both ad sets are performing within 20% CPL of each other after 30 days, consolidating into a single retargeting ad set under CBO is a valid simplification move.

---

### Pixel Events Configuration
Critical for Conversions objective — algorithm cannot optimize without reliable signal:
1. **Lead / TrialStart (Custom Event)** — fire on /trial/confirmation page. THIS IS THE OPTIMIZATION EVENT. Configure this as the conversion event in ad set settings.
2. **ViewContent** — fire on /pricing, /features, /docs page loads (feeds retargeting pool)
3. **InitiateCheckout** — fire when user clicks 'Start Free Trial' button (pre-conversion signal)
4. **Purchase** — fire on paid plan activation confirmation (future optimization target)

Enable Conversions API for server-side firing. Developer tool companies typically have engineering resources to implement CAPI — prioritize this. Browser-only pixel loses 15–25% of conversion events due to ad blockers and browser privacy settings, which is disproportionately high for a developer audience.

---

### Ad Variant Summary
| Variant ID | Headline Angle | Format | CTA |
|---|---|---|---|
| AD-001 | You checked out our pricing — here's what Pro includes | Static (product screenshot + pricing callout) | Start Free Trial |
| AD-002 | 14-day trial, no card required | Static (clean, minimalist — dev aesthetic) | Try GitPilot Free |
| AD-003 | What you missed when you left our pricing page | Carousel (3 cards: feature 1, feature 2, social proof stat) | See What's Included |

**Creative note for developer audience:** Avoid stock photography of people at computers. Use actual product UI, code snippets, or terminal output. Developer audiences have high creative fatigue with generic SaaS ads — lean into the technical authenticity.

---

### Launch Checklist
1. Verify Pixel fires 'ViewContent' on /pricing page — required to build retargeting pool
2. Confirm retargeting audience pool for pricing visitors is >= 1,000 users (check Audience Manager)
3. Confirm video viewer audience (75%+ watch time, 30 days) is >= 1,000 users
4. Implement Conversions API (CAPI) to compensate for ad blocker signal loss in developer demographic
5. Set TrialStart as the optimization event in both ad sets (not generic 'Lead')
6. Exclude converted users from all ad sets by uploading current trial/customer list as suppression audience
7. Set ABO budgets: Ad Set 1 = $65/day, Ad Set 2 = $35/day
8. Exclude Audience Network from all placements
9. Create automated rule: alert (do not auto-pause) if CPA > $120 after $200 spend — review manually
10. Schedule weekly frequency check — if average frequency exceeds 5 on either ad set, pause and refresh creative

---

### Scaling Note
At $3k/month with small retargeting pools, this campaign will hit frequency caps quickly. Plan to refresh creative every 3–4 weeks minimum. The real growth lever here is expanding the top-of-funnel (cold campaigns, content amplification) to continuously feed the retargeting pool with fresh pricing page visitors.
